subject: hid-guncon3: fix warnings.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: Compiler warnings (C89 compliance for variable declaration placement; unused return value from input_register_device). Risk of silent failure in device registration if error return value is not checked.

userspace_coupling.interface: USB device ID detection (0b9a:0800) for lightgun quirk and calibration initialization
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:5374 — device ID match triggers QUIRK_LIGHTGUN with specific calibration values

notes: Warning-fix commit is explicitly folded into carried patch 0011-hid-guncon3.patch (see patch header line 19-20). The patch documentation confirms the fix is incorporated and notes forward-port changes for usb_maxpacket() API signature (3-arg→2-arg in 6.18). The driver is MiSTer-specific (not in vanilla 6.18.38); warning fixes are essential for proper error handling of input_register_device() return value, which Main_MiSTer userspace depends on for device detection and lightgun calibration. Agrees with patch-provenance.md class D carry designation.
sonnet-verified: amended — provenance_doc_ref cited the wrong line number ('patch-provenance.md line 337', which is actually the unrelated DualSense player-LED/mute row); corrected to line 359, the actual GunCon 3 row. This is the same class of citation-contamination error flagged elsewhere in the corpus (see memory: patch-provenance.md:337 DualSense mis-citation). Diff content verified against live fork commit: variable-declaration reordering (C89 compliance) and input_register_device() return-value check both match exactly. dependency on 8179ac736 confirmed correct.
